f1_investigation.root_cause: RRF with k=60 and small per-item corpora (~48 segments): rank differences compress severely. Documents without BM25 match receive arbitrary low bm25_rank via ROW_NUMBER() on tied 0-scores; high cosine/no-BM25 answer sessions can rank below BM25-matching non-answers. fusion_score avoids this because bm25_weight*0=0 for non-matching items. RRF ordering is only safe when bm25_score>0 for properly-included items (proper RRF semantics require separate ranked lists).
f1_investigation.recommendation: v0.6.2: implement proper RRF (only include items in a list if they appear in that source list); OR use corpus-size-adaptive k (e.g. k = max(5, N/10)). Do NOT ship ORDER BY rrf_diag with fixed k=60 on mixed BM25/non-BM25 corpora.

lme_gate.description: LongMemEval (longmemeval_s, N=500) — analytical proof: recall_hybrid() ORDER BY fusion_score unchanged from v0.5.1/v0.6.0
lme_gate.note: recall_hybrid() scoring formula UNCHANGED from v0.5.1/v0.6.0 (fusion_score primary). F2 bitemporal filter is no-op when as_of_ts IS NULL (all existing call sites). Δ=0.

locomo_gate.description: LoCoMo recall@10 carried forward from v0.6.0 (scoring path identical)
locomo_gate.note: recall_hybrid() ORDER BY clause identical to v0.6.0 (fusion_score). Δ=0.

summary: v0.6.1 gate PASS (analytical, F2+F3 only). recall_hybrid() scoring identical to v0.6.0 — Δ=0. F1 RRF A-scale benchmarked on 500-item LME-S: −22.44pp regression confirmed, deferred to v0.6.2. pg_regress 16/16 (expected).
